body:
## Summary

Fixes #1.

This PR replaces the template crate with a Rust `meta-language` core: a self-describing links-network representation for lossless parsing, verification, projection, transformation, reconstruction, and versioned snapshots.

It also addresses the latest PR feedback by making competitor and language coverage executable in this PR and by keeping tests out of `src/`. Every tracked competitor/ecosystem target now has a `PARITY_FIXTURES` entry, every advertised target capability must be exercised by at least one fixture for that target, every requested Markdown/HTML/programming/natural-language target has a `LANGUAGE_FIXTURES` parse/reconstruction fixture, snapshot/versioning is tested as part of persistent syntax parity, and CI rejects Rust test markers under `src/`.

## Implementation

- Renames the crate and binary to `meta-language` / `meta_language`.
- Adds `LinkNetwork`, `Link`, `LinkId`, source spans, parse flags, metadata, verification reports, and configuration for trivia attachment and mixed-language region detection.
- Models points as self-referential links, relation links as ordinary links, field labels as explicit relation links, object identity as shared links, and concept syntax mappings for target-language reconstruction.
- Adds `LinkNetwork::parse` as the default lossless parse entry point and `reconstruct_text()` for exact reconstruction from non-missing token links.
- Adds recovery markers for unmatched parentheses while preserving original text.
- Adds mixed-region detection for Markdown fenced code and HTML regions, plus HTML script/style/style-attribute regions and content-driven fenced-code detection.
- Adds `NetworkProjection`, `LinkQuery`, `SubstitutionRule`, `TruthValue`, and concept reconstruction APIs.
- Adds immutable `NetworkSnapshot` and editable `MutableNetworkSnapshot` APIs with provenance and forward version commits.
- Adds self-description roots for `link`, `reference`, `relation link`, `language`, `grammar`, `type`, `concept`, `point`, `field`, `trivia`, `region`, and `object`.
- Adds `PARITY_TARGETS`, `PARITY_FIXTURES`, `LANGUAGE_FIXTURES`, language target registries, grammar embedding targets, and tests for all of them.
- Moves Rust tests into the `tests/` tree and adds `scripts/check-no-src-tests.rs`, workflow coverage, pre-commit coverage, and docs so `#[test]`, `#[cfg(test)]`, and test module markers under `src/` fail CI.
- Updates README, parity implementation docs, examples, and changelog fragments.

## Reproduction / Tests

The reproducing gap was that competitor coverage was previously roadmap-only, source-tree tests were not explicitly forbidden, and snapshot/versioning was named by the roadmap without an executable API. The unit tests now fail unless every `PARITY_TARGETS` entry has executable fixtures covering every advertised capability, every requested language target has a lossless fixture, core behavior works for reconstruction, recovery diagnostics, mixed regions, queries, substitutions, concept reconstruction, object identity, snapshots/version commits, and many-valued truth values, and the workflow keeps the no-`src`-tests guard wired into CI.

Local verification passed:

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `RUSTFLAGS=-Dwarnings cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- `cargo test --all-features`
- `rust-script scripts/check-no-src-tests.rs`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `rust-script scripts/check-crate-size.rs`
- `rust-script scripts/check-changelog-fragment.rs`
- `git diff --check`

## Notes

This is not a UI change, so screenshots are not included.
title: Add executable meta-language links-network core
